Fix StarRocks FE/BE/CN CPU limits to prevent LimitRange conflict

The cluster has a LimitRange that defaults CPU limit to 8 cores.
Setting CN (and BE) cpu_request to 16000m while omitting an explicit
cpu_limit caused Kubernetes to reject the StatefulSet because the
request exceeded the default limit.

Now all three specs (FE, BE, CN) explicitly set limits.cpu equal to
their cpu_request by default, overridable via a cpu_limit stack config
key.  This prevents any cluster LimitRange from silently capping CPU
below what we request.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

.NET live testing implementation (#14822)

* initial tests

* Implement .NET live and recorded test support with framework detection

- Update DotnetLanguageService.RunAllTests to set test mode env vars,
  merge live test environment variables, and use mode-based timeouts
- Add DetectTestFramework to parse test .csproj and set the correct
  env var (AZURE_TEST_MODE vs CLIENTMODEL_TEST_MODE) based on which
  test framework the package uses, with fallback to setting both
- Add PushTestAssets to push recorded assets via test-proxy after
  successful record mode runs
- Add comprehensive unit tests mirroring the JS test suite

Addresses Azure/azure-sdk-tools#14680

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove duplicate PushTestAssets, use base class implementation

The upstream LanguageService base class now has a protected virtual
PushTestAssets method (identical to our private implementation). Remove
the duplicate from DotnetLanguageService to fix CS0114 warning-as-error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use AllDirectories in DetectTestFramework to find nested csproj files

SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly missed test projects in subdirectories
(e.g. tests/MyProject/MyProject.csproj), causing framework detection
to always fall back to Unknown for nested layouts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use Name.LocalName for XML queries in DetectTestFramework

Descendants('ElementName') won't match elements in .csproj files that
declare a default XML namespace (older MSBuild-style projects). Using
Descendants().Where(e => e.Name.LocalName == ...) makes detection
resilient to both namespaced and non-namespaced project files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Prevent liveTestEnvironment from overriding test mode variables

Move liveTestEnvironment merge before the switch block so that
AZURE_TEST_MODE / CLIENTMODEL_TEST_MODE are always set last. This
prevents a caller-provided .env file from silently overriding the
requested testMode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename test to clarify it covers the Unknown-framework case

The test asserts both AZURE_TEST_MODE and CLIENTMODEL_TEST_MODE are set,
which only happens when framework detection returns Unknown. Rename and
add a comment to make the intent explicit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add early directory existence check in DetectTestFramework

Avoid exception-driven control flow when packagePath does not exist.
Return Unknown with a Debug log instead of throwing and logging a
noisy Warning.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename RunAllTests_DefaultMode_IsPlayback to clarify Unknown-framework case

Consistent with prior rename: the test uses a temp dir with no .csproj
files, so framework detection returns Unknown and both env vars are set.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor!: replace Agent/Orchestrator with Implementor, Evaluator, Validator interfaces

Introduce three specialized interfaces extending Agent<TContext, TResult>:
- Implementor<TContext>: produces code changes, returns ImplementorResult
- Evaluator<TContext>: scores changes, returns EvaluatorResult
- Validator<TContext>: gate decisions via rule(): Rule<TContext>, returns GatekeeperResult

All agents are now class instances implementing one of the three interfaces
(or base Agent for SynthesizerAgent). Zero free agent functions remain.

Key changes:
- Rule<A> is now fully async: (ctx: A) => Promise<Tri>
- Gate.lift() converts sync lambdas to async rules
- Gate combinators (and/or/not) await internally
- New llmAsJudge<A>() factory creates LLM-backed Validators
- Orchestrator interface removed; orchestrators implement their role

BREAKING CHANGE: Orchestrator interface removed. Rule<A> signature changed
from sync to async. All runXxx() free functions replaced by class instances.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: search_memory skill — cross-conversation message history search (#236)

- Add SearchMemorySkill that searches message_embeddings semantically
  - Required param: query (String)
  - Optional params: conversationId (String), topK (Int, default 5)
  - conversationId=null → cross-conversation search across all past messages
  - conversationId provided → scoped search for summary-to-detail retrieval
  - Returns role + content + date + conversationId prefix per result
  - Registered via @IntoSet in SkillsModule

- Add RagRepository.searchMessages() method powering the skill
  - Uses existing embeddingEngine + vectorStore + messageDao/embeddingDao
  - MAX_DISTANCE=0.4 filter consistent with existing message retrieval

- Add MessageSearchResult data class in rag package

- Add MemorySearchResult.conversationId (nullable) populated from
  EpisodicMemoryEntity for episodic results

- Update RagRepository.getRelevantContext() to prefix [Episodic Memories]
  lines with 'conversation:ID |' so the model can call search_memory with
  the conversationId for summary-to-detail detail retrieval

Closes #236

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: batch message lookups in searchMessages and getRelevantContext

Replace N individual getByConversation() calls with a single getByIds()
batch query. Avoids loading entire conversations to find individual
messages — O(N) DB round trips → O(1).

commit.message:
Enhance nightly CI configuration to align with Rock submodule bump PR (#4202)

## Motivation

Part of shift left so we can run extended test for schduled builds
(nightly builds) to match the bump PR testing.

https://amd.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/MLSE/pages/1517193184/BUMP+PR+-+Faster+Failure+Triage

## Technical Details
Added support for a unified "nightly" job in the project map, ensuring
full test coverage across Linux and Windows environments. Removed
unnecessary subtree evaluations for nightly runs in the CI workflow and
map it to ci_nightly case

Summary of changes that align the public-rocm-sys nightly with TheRock
submodule-bump PR test coverage:

### 1. **`therock_configure_ci.py`** – Nightly uses explicit “nighlty”
config
- **Early return for nightly:** If `is_nightly` (event is `schedule`),
the script now returns a single job from `project_map["nightly"]` with:
  - `cmake_options`: `-DTHEROCK_ENABLE_ALL=ON`
- `projects_to_test`: the full list (hip-tests, rocrtst, aqlprofile,
rocprofiler-compute, rocprofiler-systems, rocr-debug-agent, rocgdb,
rocprim, rocthrust, rocrand, hiprand, hipblaslt, rocblas, hipblas,
rocroller, miopen, miopenprovider, hipfft, rocfft, rocsparse, hipsparse,
hipsparselt, rocsolver, hipsolver, rocwmma)
- **Effect:** Nightly no longer depends on `modified_paths` or subtree
merging, so:
- Linux and Windows both run the full “nightly” job (no path-based
Windows skip on schedule).
- Test set is explicitly the same as TheRock submodule-bump PRs
(rocm-systems scope).
- **Cleanup:** Removed the old “Scheduled run → evaluate all subtrees”
branch, since nightly is handled at the top.

### 2. **`therock_matrix.py`** – Alignment comment
- Documented that the `"nightly"` entry is the same test coverage as
TheRock submodule-bump PRs and that nightly uses this entry for
alignment.

### 3. **`therock-ci-nightly.yml`** – Workflow fix
- **Removed invalid reference:** `SUBTREES: ${{
steps.detect.outputs.subtrees }}` was removed from both “Determine
Linux/Windows projects” steps because there is no `detect` step (so it
was always empty).
- Schedule runs ignore `SUBTREES` (nightly uses `project_map["all"]`).
For `workflow_dispatch`, selection is driven by `PROJECTS`
(`inputs.projects`), so dropping `SUBTREES` does not change behavior.

**Result:** Nightly (schedule) in public-rocm-sys now runs one Linux and
one Windows job with the “nightly” config for scheduled builds and the
same `projects_to_test` list as submodule-bump PRs in therock, and the
workflow no longer references a missing step.

* feat: Phase 2 — Drive & Charge Session sub-FSMs with 41+27 tests

Hierarchical sub-FSMs owned by the Vehicle FSM Driving/Charging states.
Each ensures data quality through staged snapshot capture and validation.

Drive Session FSM (internal/fsm/drive/):
- States: Pending → Active → Ending → Completed (+ Recovered)
- PENDING waits for start snapshot (odometer, battery, location)
- ENDING waits up to 60s for end snapshot
- COMPLETED validates: distance>0, efficiency 100-600 Wh/mi, net energy>0
- Catches real production bugs (Drive #24: 9 Wh/mi, -479 Wh net)
- 27 tests (state transitions + validation)

Charge Session FSM (internal/fsm/charge/):
- States: Pending → Active → Completing → Done (+ Recovered)
- PENDING waits for start battery + location + charger info
- COMPLETING waits up to 30s for end battery + energy
- Validates: end_battery >= start, energy > 0, rate < 350 kW
- 24 tests (state transitions + validation)

Both sub-FSMs support:
- Staged snapshot capture (don't start until data is ready)
- Continuous end-value updates during Active (always have latest)
- ForceComplete for stale timeout
- RecoverFrom for pod restart reconstruction
- Validation that WARNS but never blocks persistence

Total: 92 FSM tests across vehicle + drive + charge packages.

- fsm_instance_id: links to drive_id, charge_session_id, etc.

- context_snapshot: JSONB with type-specific data at transition time

Wires the new FSM alongside the old state machine in shadow mode.

but does NOT write to vehicle_states/vehicles tables.

What it does:

- Compares resulting state with old vehicleStateMachine's state
- Logs DISCREPANCY warnings when they disagree (with both states)
- Logs transitions to fsm_transitions table (marked shadow:true)
- Handles timeout comparison when vehicles go offline

New files:

New API endpoints:

* feat: Phase 8 — Notification Cooldown + Delivery FSMs with 14 tests

Alert Cooldown FSM (internal/fsm/notification/cooldown.go):
- States: Armed → Fired → Suppressed
- Configurable cooldown duration (default 15 min) and max fires per hour (default 4)
- ShouldFire() returns true/false — caller creates notification only on true
- Prevents notification spam (e.g., 'battery low' every 30 seconds)
- 5 tests covering fire, suppression, expiry, rate limit, stats

Notification Delivery FSM (internal/fsm/notification/delivery.go):
- States: Created → Sending → Delivered/Partial/Failed → Retrying → Dead
- Per-channel tracking (push, email, webhook) with individual status
- Exponential backoff retry (2s, 4s, 8s) with max 3 retries
- Partial state when some channels succeed and others fail
- Failed channels reset to pending on retry, delivered channels kept
- 9 tests covering all states, retry, backoff, channel reset

Migration 000046: notifications + alert_cooldown_state tables
- notifications: id, vehicle_id, type, title, body, severity, state, channels JSONB,
  signals_snapshot, retry_count, delivered_at, next_retry_at
- alert_cooldown_state: per (rule_id, vehicle_id) cooldown tracking

Total: 106 FSM tests across all packages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Phase 9 — Command Execution FSM with 11 tests

Command FSM (internal/fsm/command/machine.go):
- States: Queued → Waking → WakeConfirmed → Sending → Succeeded/Failed/TimedOut → Retrying → GaveUp
- Auto wake-then-execute: detects asleep vehicle, wakes, waits, then sends command
- Wake retry: up to 2 retries before giving up (30s timeout per attempt)
- Command retry: exponential backoff (2s, 4s, 8s) for retryable errors (429, 500, timeout)
- Non-retryable errors (401 auth, 400 bad request) fail immediately → GaveUp
- StatusMessage() returns user-friendly strings for SSE toasts
- 11 tests covering happy path, wake failures, command failures, retries, status messages

Migration 000047: command_executions table with full lifecycle tracking
- state, wake_retry_count, retry_count, last_error JSONB, tesla_response JSONB
- Timestamps: created_at, wake_sent_at, wake_confirmed_at, command_sent_at, completed_at

Total: 117 FSM tests across 5 packages (vehicle + drive + charge + notification + command).

commit.message:
feat(compiler): caller-save convention for non-tail recursive functions

Before each CALL, the compiler now emits LOAD instructions to save
locals[2..ns-1] onto the value stack. After CALL returns, STORE
instructions restore them. This preserves local variables across
recursive calls without any VM changes.

New worklist tags:
  17 (SAVE_LOCALS ns): emits LOAD 2, LOAD 3, ..., LOAD ns-1
  18 (RESTORE_LOCALS ns): emits STORE ns (temp), STORE ns-1..2, LOAD ns

Applied to all CALL sites: self-recursive, 1-arg function table,
and 2-arg function table calls.

Fixes: quicksort now correctly sorts (10,7,8,9,1,5) -> (1,5,7,8,9,10).
Binary: 248,178 -> 251,258 bytes. Fixed point verified.

commit.message:
Handle reserved keywords used as DuckDB cast type names (fixes ::filter[] parse failure) (#41)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Fix `jarify lint` and `jarify fmt` crashing on SQL files that use DuckDB
reserved keywords as user-defined type names in cast expressions (e.g.
`[]::filter[]`).

## Root cause

DuckDB allows user-defined types whose names are SQL reserved words.
`filter` is a common example — teams create a `filter` struct type
alongside DuckDB's built-in `FILTER (WHERE ...)` aggregate clause.
sqlglot tokenises `filter` as a `FILTER` keyword token rather than an
identifier, so `[]::filter[]` fails with `Expected type` regardless of
context.

## Fix

Add a pre-processing step in `src/jarify/parser.py` that quotes reserved
keywords in cast type-name positions before handing the SQL to sqlglot:

| Stage | SQL |
|---|---|
| Input | `COALESCE(x, []::filter[])` |
| Parser input | `COALESCE(x, []::"filter"[])` |
| Generator output | `COALESCE(x, []::filter[])` ✓ |

The set of keywords needing quoting is computed **once per process (~0.4
ms)** using sqlglot's own tokeniser: any keyword whose token type is
neither `TokenType.VAR` nor a member of `Parser.TYPE_TOKENS` is quoted.
The result is cached via `lru_cache`.

## Verification

- `get_programs.sql` (the original repro file) now lints cleanly — only
real style warnings, zero parse errors.
- 109 tests pass, ruff clean.
- New fixture `duckdb/reserved_keyword_type_cast` covers the
`::filter[]`-in-CASE pattern.
- New parser unit tests cover quoting, non-quoting, already-quoted, and
real-world patterns.

Resolves #37

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix fee calculation parity bugs with regression tests

Fix 3 consensus-breaking fee calculation divergences from stellar-core:

1. envelope_num_ops: add +1 for fee-bump wrapper (Fixes #1497)
   stellar-core's getNumOperations() counts the fee-bump wrapper as an
   operation. This affected compare_tx_sets, tx_applying_fee, and queue
   admission fee-rate calculations.

2. compute_better_fee: reject equal-rate transactions (Fixes #1496)
   Changed != Greater to == Less so equal fee rates fall through to
   compute the required minimum fee, matching stellar-core's strict
   comparison.

3. Soroban base fee: compute after parallel build (Fixes #1477, #1494)
   Moved soroban_base_fee computation to after build_parallel_soroban_phase
   so it reflects surviving transactions, not dropped candidates.
   Added had_tx_not_fitting feedback from the parallel builder matching
   stellar-core's hadTxNotFittingLane mechanism.

Closes #1513

commit.message:
Frontend: MBO as source of truth, compute MBP on render

Replace incremental MBP maintenance (addToLevel/removeFromLevel) with
pure MBO storage. The orders Map is the single source of truth.
renderBook() computes price levels on-the-fly by aggregating from
the orders map each frame.

This eliminates drift caused by conflation dropping intermediate
updates (e.g. Update+Delete conflated to Delete — client removed
from stale price level). With MBO, Add/Update is a simple map.set,
Delete is map.delete — always correct regardless of conflation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
